qa.overall_assessment: Both blocking objections from iteration 1 are fully addressed in the updated BA spec. OBJ-1: AC3b added at lines 166-173 of the spec, explicitly covering the single-candidate fail-closed branch (commit.md line 161). OBJ-2: AC4 verification recipe rewritten to a stable pinned form targeting the specific task-id and specific false-positive files. All 5 dimensions re-checked and pass. Codex adversarial review confirmed PASS with no PROPOSED_FIX findings.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Marker-placement obligation for future spec authors is not enforced by any mechanism. Stage 3 will silently produce empty candidate sets for task-ids where the spec author forgot to add the marker. BA acknowledges this as future-cycle debt (Edge Cases section). Codex also flagged this as OBSERVATION_ONLY. Not a blocker for this cycle.

Line numbers in the spec reference 'line 157' as the change location. Line numbers in Markdown command files can drift if other lines are inserted before this location. The content predicate (the grep fragment) is a stable anchor for locating the change. Codex flagged this as OBSERVATION_ONLY. Not a blocker.

A code-fenced or example occurrence of the exact marker string inside a spec would still match grep -lF; preventing that would require fence-aware or anchored parsing. This is adjacent hardening beyond the requested grep replacement. Codex raised this as OBSERVATION_ONLY.

OBJ-1.iteration_1_title: AC3 covers only multi-candidate fail-closed path; single-candidate path not verified
OBJ-1.resolution_evidence: AC3b added at ticket-20260522-080646-C.md lines 166-173. GIVEN exactly one marker-bearing spec, WHEN stage 3 collects candidates, THEN single-candidate outcome fires: print 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context: <path>' and exit non-zero. Verification recipe: grep -nF 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' commands/commit.md returns >=1 match. QA ran this recipe and confirmed match at line 161, exit 0. R3 in context JSON also updated to reference both AC3 and AC3b.

OBJ-2.iteration_1_title: AC4 verification recipe is time-transient and will produce a false-pass once any future spec acquires the marker
OBJ-2.resolution_evidence: AC4 now uses stable form: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md returns empty (exit 1). Spec explicitly notes this replaces prior time-transient 'grep -rF spec-continuation-of docs/dev/specs/'. QA ran the stable recipe and confirmed exit 1 (no matches). Recipe is permanently stable because it pins the specific incident task-id and specific false-positive files; future specs use different task-ids.

findings: BA claims commands/commit.md line 157 contains 'grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md' — QA confirmed via direct read; exact text present.

BA claims the incident occurred in cycle 20260519-211515 with two false-positive specs — QA confirmed spec-20260518-225715.md has 3 prose references (lines 233, 235, 245); spec-20260520-221059.md has 7 prose references (lines 87, 90, 91, 132, 133, 134, 181). Both counts match BA claims.
BA claims spec-20260520-221059.md:87 documents the incident as 'D2 — Step 7 false positive' — QA confirmed exact text at that line.
BA claims neither false-positive file contains a spec-continuation-of marker — QA confirmed: grep -cF of marker returns 0 for both files.
Context JSON observations[] entries are factual (direct reads and measured grep counts) despite evidence_type label 'inferred' — minor label mismatch, not a blocker.

User requirement (verbatim from context JSON requirement.original): 'OOS item C — commit.md Step 7 ambiguous spec candidate. Stage 3 uses a literal grep of the task-id string across all docs/dev/specs/*.md files, which returns false positives. The fix is to tighten the candidate predicate: only a spec file that contains a machine-readable marker should be a candidate.'
BA scopes the change to ONE grep pattern replacement at ONE location (commands/commit.md:157). No other files, no other lines.
BA's Won't Have list correctly excludes: stage 1, stage 2, fail-closed policy, retroactive marker injection, any other file or line.
No universal scope claims ('all', 'every') in user requirement that BA has narrowed — requirement is already scoped to a single component.
Marker format `<!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> -->` preserved verbatim from user requirement.

OBJ-1 resolved: BA has now investigated BOTH fail-closed branches. AC3 covers multi-candidate (line 162) and AC3b covers single-candidate (line 161). Both branches structurally affected by the predicate change are now explicitly verified.
BA investigated structural separation of stage 3 predicate from stage 4 outcome — confirmed these are in separate numbered paragraphs (3) and (4) in commit.md.
BA investigated retroactive compatibility — confirmed no existing spec has the marker, addressed gracefully via AC4.
Context JSON development_approach.validation_approach explicitly enumerates all four verification checks including AC3b.
commands/commit.md exists at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/commands/commit.md (confirmed).
Step 7 stage-3 is at lines 156-157. The grep predicate 'grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md' is confirmed at line 157.
Only ONE occurrence of grep.*TASK_ID exists in the entire commit.md file — BA's scope claim is confirmed.
Fail-closed multi-candidate text confirmed at line 162: 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage: <paths>; explicit context.spec_path required'.
Fail-closed single-candidate text confirmed at line 161: 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context: <path>'.
Both false-positive spec files (spec-20260518-225715.md, spec-20260520-221059.md) exist and contain zero marker occurrences.

OBJ-2 resolved: AC4 verification recipe is now stable pinned form: grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->' docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md. QA ran this recipe, exit 1 confirmed.
AC1 verification recipe (also targets same pinned files/task-id): exit 1 confirmed.
AC3 verification recipe: grep -nF 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage' commands/commit.md — confirmed match at line 162, exit 0.
AC3b verification recipe: grep -nF 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' commands/commit.md — confirmed match at line 161, exit 0.

AC5 verification recipe: pre-dev-fix state correct — 'literal-task-id grep' present at line 157 (exit 0); 'machine-readable marker' not yet present (exit 1). These are EXPECTED before dev applies the fix.
AC2 verification: cannot pre-verify (requires marker-bearing fixture); spec text is clear and leaves this to dev to demonstrate with a fixture or the first correctly-marked spec.
No spec text vs execution drift detected across any AC.

verification_recipes_run.recipe: grep -nF 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage' commands/commit.md
verification_recipes_run.output: 162:    - If set has multiple elements: print `multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage: <paths>; explicit context.spec_path required` and exit non-zero (fail-closed).

verification_recipes_run.recipe: grep -nF 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' commands/commit.md
verification_recipes_run.output: 161:    - If set has exactly one element: print `spec produced this cycle but not linked in context: <path>` and exit non-zero (fail-closed).
157: ... literal-task-id grep (present, as expected before dev fix)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex examined the updated BA spec adversarially. Verdict: PASS upheld. No PROPOSED_FIX findings issued. Codex confirmed: AC3b present at ticket-20260522-080646-C.md:166-173 covering single-candidate branch from commit.md:161; AC4 now stable/pinned at ticket-20260522-080646-C.md:175-183 using exact marker + exact known false-positive files; exact replacement specified at ticket lines 91-94 and 201-203; multi/single fail-closed outcomes remain structurally separate at commit.md:161-162. Three OBSERVATION_ONLY items: (1) commit.md:157 still has old predicate in current worktree — not a failure in BA-validation/pre-dev context; (2) future marker production by spec writers remains outside this ticket (acknowledged as future-cycle debt); (3) code-fenced occurrence of marker string would still match grep -lF — adjacent hardening beyond this requested change.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All three OBSERVATION_ONLY items pre-acknowledged in spec. No changes to verdict required. Codex session id: 019e4ee6-e1fe-7c02-814c-80bae22f373f.

summary.notes: Both OBJ-1 and OBJ-2 from iteration 1 are fully resolved. AC3b explicitly covers the single-candidate fail-closed branch. AC4 uses a permanently stable pinned verification recipe. All 5 BA-validation dimensions pass. Codex adversarial review found no PROPOSED_FIX issues. The BA spec is cleared for dev to proceed with the single-line predicate replacement at commands/commit.md:157.
